subject: PGlite migration idempotency fix in packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts
executive_summary: The idempotency fix is a strict improvement that adds migration tracking where none existed before. 27 tests pass (19 existing + 8 lab-rat). The security-adversary identified one finding they consider blocking (no transaction wrapping around per-migration DDL+INSERT), but this is a PRE-EXISTING architectural gap — the fix actually narrows the blast radius by skipping already-successful migrations on retry and only re-running the failed one. The TRUSTED SQL from drizzle-kit-generated migration files is committed to git; the risk of a multi-statement migration partially applying is identical before and after the fix.

disputed_finding.security_adversary_claim: BLOCKING — No transaction wrapping means partial DDL failure leaves database unrecoverable
disputed_finding.qa_assessment: NOT BLOCKING — This gap pre-exists the fix. Before the fix, ALL migrations re-ran on every call (including the partially-applied one). After the fix, ONLY the failed migration re-runs. The fix strictly reduces blast radius. Transaction wrapping is a valid follow-up improvement but not a regression.
disputed_finding.resolution: PASS with note. The idempotency fix is additive; the transaction-wrapping gap is a separate architectural slice.

source_code_review.detail: 20 net lines added to PGlite branch. node-postgres else block untouched.

test_migration_pg.detail: 3/3 pass — includes dedicated idempotency test with hash count verification.

test_lab_rat.detail: 8/8 pass — partial failure, hash tracking, param safety, repeated calls, all-skipped loop.

test_bisect_skip.detail: skipMigrations:true not wired. Confirmed pre-existing via git stash baseline.

type_safety.detail: Zero type errors. All signatures unchanged. 5 callers compatible.

sql_injection.detail: INSERT parameterized ($1,$2). hash is hex-only. Migration SQL from committed drizzle-kit files.

race_condition.detail: PGlite single-connection WASM. Sequential for...of with await.

transaction_atomicity.detail: Pre-existing gap. Fix improves situation by narrowing retry scope. Transaction wrapping recommended as follow-up.

table_namespace.detail: public schema vs drizzle schema — cosmetic.

partial_failure_recovery.detail: Lab-rat test #3 proves: failed migration hash NOT recorded → retried on next call → succeeds after fix.

lab_rat_tests.name: fresh database creates tracking table + applies all
second call is no-op, hash count stays at 4
partial failure — hash not recorded, retried successfully
pre-existing empty tracking table — applies all
clear rows — skip depends on hash presence
three consecutive calls — tracking table survives
no migration files — empty-loop no crash

security_adversary_findings.verdict: ADVISORY — Pre-existing gap. Fix improves blast radius. Transaction wrapping recommended.
security_adversary_findings.rationale: The gap pre-exists the fix. Before: ALL migrations re-ran (including partially-applied). After: ONLY the failed migration re-runs. The fix is strictly better. Transaction wrapping is a separate architectural improvement.

pre_existing_issues.detail: Returns healthy when pool is undefined
applyMigrations lacks per-migration transaction boundary. Pre-existing; fix reduces retry blast radius.

recommendations: ✅ The idempotency fix is correct and ready.
📋 Wrap per-migration DDL+INSERT in a transaction (BEGIN/COMMIT/ROLLBACK) — separate slice.
📋 Use INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to simplify SELECT elimination — low priority.
📋 Move tracking table to drizzle.__drizzle_migrations — cosmetic.
🔧 Await applyMigrations in adapter.ts instead of fire-and-forget.
